auth_notes: Requires SMART on FHIR backend-to-backend authentication. This involves generating a signed JWT (RS256) using a private key, exchanging it for an access token via Epic's OAuth2 endpoint, and registering the public key in the Epic Developer Portal.
rate_limits: Varies by endpoint and specific Epic instance; typically enforced via concurrent request limits and daily quotas per client ID.
gotchas: Strict HIPAA compliance requirements mean data must be encrypted in transit and at rest within n8n.
Epic's FHIR implementation varies slightly between versions (e.g., DSTU2 vs R4), requiring careful resource mapping.
Testing requires access to the Epic Sandbox (App Orchard) which has different data than production environments.
client_must_provide: Specific FHIR Resource Scopes (e.g., Patient.Read, Observation.Write)
research_notes: The integration must be built using the HTTP Request node. A pre-processing step (likely a Code node) is required to generate the RS256 JWT assertion. The workflow must handle token refreshing manually as Epic tokens are short-lived. All data structures must follow the HL7 FHIR standard.

effort_recommendation.rationale: The integration must be built using the HTTP Request node. A pre-processing step (likely a Code node) is required to generate the RS256 JWT assertion. The workflow must handle token refreshing manually as Epic tokens are short-lived. All data structures must follow the HL7 FHIR standard.
